title: Control of Unit 02 in End of Evangelion
display_title: Control of Unit 02 in End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Control of Unit-02 in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Jun 26, 2007 5:53 am This was initially brought up in the Rei's Birth thread, and I thought it was worth getting into again. http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=83472#83472 I personally agree with BrikHaus in that it really dosen't seem like Kyoko is the one controlling Asuka's actions in Unit-02, yet many folks around here seem to throw it around like it's a proven fact. And thus, I'd really like to see where everyone stands on this issue. Eva Yojimbo wrote: For me, I'm content that what we see is more of a joining of Asuka and Kyoko. Rather than either being entirely in control, it's one of those "OMG! Outrageously high synch ratio!" kinda things.

[]: Re: Control of Unit-02 in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 26, 2007 6:29 am TriLink12 wrote: I personally agree with BrikHaus in that it really dosen't seem like Kyoko is the one controlling Asuka's actions in Unit-02, yet many folks around here seem to throw it around like it's a proven fact.

[]: Re: Control of Unit-02 in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jun 26, 2007 7:39 am Reichu wrote: I'd been under the impression that "Bridge Theory" was so impossibly unpopular it could never give the impression of having "many" adherents. Weird...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 26, 2007 10:48 am Could someone sum up this "Bridge Theory" for me? I don't wanna read through 7 pages of a thread I think I already read a while back...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:06 pm The kernel of it is that Asuka was simply acting as a medium for Kyoko during the UBF.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:37 pm Upon much reflection I don't think this is possible. The nature of the process by default gives Asuka influence over what happens. That's not to say Kyoko didn't do some really cool looking things that Asuka simply can't, energy blast and reactivation to name a few, but by that same token Kyoko doesn't strike me as a swordsman and so on. A semi-legit team effort is the only thing that makes sense give all the facts.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 26, 2007 5:41 pm BRIDGE THEORY BY REICHU: [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=69669#69669[/url] wrote: The concept is sort of convoluted, but the Eva-Pilot stuff was convoluted in the first place. The most basic part of the premise is that Kyoko is using Asuka in some way to bridge the gap between her core and body -- since Asuka can "bypass" the restraints, and Kyoko, apparently, can't. (But, hey. Even Bardiel needed Toji.) This is to account for various things that have been mentioned multiple times already. The exact details of how the "bridging" works are fairly abstract, but Asuka is said to be acting in the capacity of a "deeply immersed spectator" more than anything else, and influenced heavily by "feedback", mental contamination, and the very thing she does best (self-delusion). (And don't forget that regression.) Of course, this whole idea is roughly 90% unpopular around these parts, due to the fact that it is blatantly, obviously, and verifiably wrong. I mean, DUH.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jun 26, 2007 6:21 pm Again there are things that Kyoko wouldn't be able to do by herself like activating the on-board weapons and Kyoko reall lll y doesn't strike me as someone trained in close-combat tactics, I mean she was a freaking bio-engineer for crying out loud. We never see Yui going nuts with a sword or any weapons for that matter. In fact the closest we get to that is Yui ripping Sachiel's rib off and beating the core with it. Watch berserk Yui and EVA-00 the watch Kyoko in EoE and it becomes clear Asuka is doing some of what you see. At the same time there are quite a few things that Asuka wouldn't be capable of either. Take from that what you will. What Kyoko wanted is, sadly, open for interpritation. Most of the time she sounds like a loving mother, but this isn't what she was like when she was alive. Kyoko was a very poor excuse for a mother and no noble acts in the final battle will ever make up for this. Then one must factor in the "please die with me" screeches, not exactly a trustworthy person if you ask me. There seems to be a tug-of-war foing on in her head with the maternal part winning most of the time, but we're left with the question of weather or not the nutty Kyoko surfaced anywhere in that fight.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jun 26, 2007 11:39 pm So it sounds like the "Bridge Theory" is just a hunch that's not really based on anything? If that's the case, then I have to agree with Trilink's assessment when he said: TriLink wrote: It struck me as kinda being like "OMG! With Momma on my side, there's no way I can lose!". As if all Asuka needed was that little bit of reassurance from her mother to get her confidence back.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 27, 2007 8:41 am BrikHaus wrote: So it sounds like the "Bridge Theory" is just a hunch that's not really based on anything?
